steps.title: Map the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1 says envelope-shaped paper bags were impractical for carrying goods. The sentence after the blank says these same bags dominated the market because they were the only ones that could be mass-produced. Being impractical would normally hurt sales, so dominating the market is the opposite of what we'd expect.

steps.title: Pick the transition that signals contrast
steps.content: The blank needs a transition that flags this unexpected, opposing outcome — a contrast word like "However".

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — However,: signals the contrast between impracticality and market dominance. ✓
  • Choice B — For instance,: wrong relationship — the next sentence isn't an example of impracticality, it's the opposite outcome. ✗
  • Choice C — Thus,: wrong relationship — being impractical wouldn't cause the bags to dominate the market, so cause-and-effect doesn't fit. ✗
  • Choice D — In other words,: wrong relationship — market dominance is not a restatement of impracticality. ✗
That is the correct answer.
